mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its medium-term trend indicates accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of crossing events over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with accommodative policy rates creates stagflationary expectations, compressing real discount rates and corporate margins to produce negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20d returns in >65% of occurrences, contradicting the stagflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to stronger corporate earnings growth, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 valuations and generates positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll additions reflect robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns when the economy operates above potential output.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive returns when the output gap is positive, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in high-output-gap regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes >0.4% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.
